title: Yes, no, that's right
pedagogy: Vocabulary: ใช่ (low ช + mai ek → falling), ไม่ (low ม + mai ek → falling), ได้ (mid ด + mai tho → falling). Concept: Thai has no universal 'yes' — verb questions are answered by echoing the verb; ใช่ confirms identity questions only. Negation pattern ไม่ + X introduced with sentence_build; ไม่/ไม้/ใหม่/ไหม minimal pairs from the tone-pair bank.

exercises.title: Thai has no word for “yes”
body: Ask a Thai speaker “do you want it?” and the answer is the verb again: เอา (want) or ไม่เอา (not want). The question hands you the word to answer with.
ใช่ only confirms “is it X?” questions — “is this the bus?” ใช่. Using it for everything is a learner tell.
And ครับ/ค่ะ on its own works as a soft “yes, I hear you” — the particles from Greetings keep paying rent.

exercises.explanation: ไม่ negates whatever follows it: ไม่ใช่ = “not so”. Same pattern for every verb you'll ever learn.
ได้ครับ is the everyday “sure thing” — you'll hear it from every waiter, driver, and receptionist in Thailand.
